mnemonics: B-Voting: Bagging, Boosting, Voting. Think of a 'B-Vote' to elect the best model.
keyPoints: Ensemble methods combine multiple models to improve performance and reduce overfitting.
Bagging (e.g., Random Forest) trains models in parallel on bootstrapped samples to reduce variance.
Boosting (e.g., AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting) trains models sequentially, focusing on previous errors to reduce bias.
commonMistakes: Assuming more models always lead to better performance, ignoring increased complexity and overfitting risk.
Using bagging and boosting interchangeably without understanding their different purposes (variance vs. bias reduction).
